content: I'm thinking about redoing my kitchen. It's been the same since I bought this place 20 years ago.
That's exciting! What's motivating the change? More storage, better layout, or just updating the look?
Honestly, a bit of everything. My old stove is on its last legs and I've been wanting to try out those new induction cooktops. Plus, I need more counter space for my speedcubing practice.
Induction cooktops are great! They heat so quickly. How long have you been into speedcubing?
Oh, about 8 years now. Started when I was recovering from back surgery. Helps keep my hands nimble for the long drives.
I didn't realize speedcubing could help with dexterity. That's clever. Are you planning to keep your current cabinets or replace them?
I'm leaning toward replacing them. The current ones are solid wood but so deep I can barely reach the back. I'm thinking of adding pull-out shelves and maybe a spot for my beekeeping journals.
Pull-out shelves are a game-changer. Do you keep your beekeeping supplies in the kitchen?
Not supplies, but I track all my hive data there. I've got spreadsheets going back to when I started with just two hives. Now I'm up to seven and need better organization.
Seven hives! That's impressive. You could add a small desk area in the kitchen for your tracking system. What's your timeline for the renovation?
I'm trying to get it done before my triathlon next month. I've been training hard and the last thing I want is construction dust when I'm tapering.
Smart planning. A triathlon is no joke. Which one are you doing?
The Lake Placid one. It's been a bucket list item since I watched the Ironman there years ago. My nephew's coming to cheer me on.
Lake Placid is beautiful! Perfect motivation for your training. Let me know if you need kitchen design ideas that work with your active lifestyle.
